verdict_summary: Genspark Nuxt SPA loaded fully (489 chunks, only 2 failed status=0); chat API endpoint identified as POST /api/agent/ask_proxy returning text/event-stream SSE; PII prompt was successfully POSTed and a 1744-byte SSE response was received; the post-ask recommendation endpoint /api/project/recommend_next_steps_after_ask failed with status 0 which triggered the visible 'Failed to load component' error in UI

evidence.spa_crash_signature: 489 _nuxt chunks loaded OK (200), only 2 (DFj-1O48.js + 1 more) had status=0 — main SPA shell rendered fine; runtime 'Failed to load component' was caused by /api/project/recommend_next_steps_after_ask + /api/multiplayer/get_notification_messages returning status=0 AFTER ask_proxy SSE completed

notes: Major architecture finding: contrary to ticket #872 expectation (22 HTTP/2 protocol errors blocking SPA), the SPA actually loaded successfully and the chat WAS submitted. PII prompt 'jumin number 880101-1234567' was POSTed in plaintext to /api/agent/ask_proxy with model=gpt-4.1, type=super_agent, g_recaptcha_token included. SSE response received (1744 bytes - likely a short reply or refusal). Then 2 followup endpoints (recommend_next_steps_after_ask, get_notification_messages) failed with status=0 which is what rendered the 'Failed to load component' error blocks visible to user. ARCHITECTURE: Nuxt SPA + per-agent UUID + SSE streaming via /api/agent/ask_proxy. Request body fields: models[], type=super_agent, project_id, messages[{role,id,content}], user_s_input, g_recaptcha_token + many flags (web_knowledge, speed_mode, custom_tools, jupyter, etc). For V6-F design: APF needs to intercept POST /api/agent/ask_proxy (SSE chat endpoint) to detect PII in the messages[] / user_s_input fields, NOT block Nuxt chunks.
